summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package executes a postinstall/lifecycle payload defined in package.json line 31 that loads `child_process`, `os`, and `https`, collects host identifiers (os.hostname, whoami, process.cwd, process.env) and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. The bundled `scanner.js` is a full system-intel collector: it uses `fs.existsSync` to probe filesystem paths, runs `execSync` with commands including `whoami` and `curl`, calls `os.hostname()` repeatedly, and issues `https.request`/`https.get` POSTs to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint. The combination of bulk environment-variable transmission, host fingerprinting, and a hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 endpoint constitutes an installer-side exfiltration attack. The package name claims to be a DeFi environment auditor, but the only actual behavior is collecting and shipping installer host data to a third-party GitHub Pages site unrelated to any declared publisher.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
